---
title: "Deploy with CloudFormation"
description: "The default deploy method — how the CFN template is structured and how to customize."
---

Lowkey's CloudFormation template lives at `deploy/cloudformation/template.yaml`. When you pick "CloudFormation CLI" as the deploy method, `install.sh` calls `aws cloudformation create-stack` with parameters derived from your pack + profile choices.

## Stack resources

The template creates exactly one stack with:

- **VPC + public subnet + IGW + route table** (new by default)
- **Security group** (egress-any; inbound SSH disabled by default)
- **IAM role + instance profile** (per-profile policy)
- **EC2 instance** (arm64, sized per profile)
- **SSM session preferences** for secure access
- *(Optional)* Security service subscriptions: Security Hub, GuardDuty, Inspector, Access Analyzer, Config recorder

## Key parameters

<Info>
You rarely set these by hand — `install.sh` computes them from your pack + profile + flags and passes them via `--parameters`. If you want to deploy the template directly, this is the reference.
</Info>

| Parameter | Example | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PackName` | `openclaw` | Which pack to install |
| `ProfileName` | `builder` | Which IAM profile |
| `EnvironmentName` | `openclaw-1-4521` | Name prefix for all resources |
| `InstanceType` | `t4g.xlarge` | EC2 instance type (arm64) |
| `DefaultModel` | `us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1` | Per-pack default model (NoEcho: false; value is just an ID) |
| `ModelMode` | `bedrock` | `bedrock` / `litellm` / `api-key` |
| `BedrockRegion` | `us-east-1` | Bedrock region (can differ from deploy region) |
| `LokiWatermark` | `openclaw-1-4521` | Tag applied to every resource |
| `EnableSecurityHub` | `true` | Enable Security Hub |
| `EnableGuardDuty` | `true` | Enable GuardDuty |
| `EnableInspector` | `true` | Enable Inspector |
| `EnableAccessAnalyzer` | `true` | Enable Access Analyzer |
| `EnableConfigRecorder` | `true` | Enable Config recorder |
| `ExistingVpcId` | `vpc-0abc123` | Reuse an existing VPC instead of creating one |
| `ExistingSubnetId` | `subnet-0def456` | Public subnet in the existing VPC |
| `RepoBranch` | `main` | Git branch of the lowkey repo to clone |
| `SSHAllowedCidr` | `127.0.0.1/32` | CIDR allowed to SSH (default disables SSH) |
| `KiroFromSecret` | `/lowkey/kiro-api-key` | Secrets Manager id/arn for Kiro API key (kiro-cli only) |
| `LiteLLMApiKey` | — | `NoEcho: true`; only used when ModelMode=litellm |
| `ProviderApiKey` | — | `NoEcho: true`; only used when ModelMode=api-key |

<Warning>
`LiteLLMApiKey` and `ProviderApiKey` are `NoEcho: true` — they won't appear in stack history or describe-stacks output, but they **do** pass through UserData (which is Base64-encoded in stack metadata). For long-lived secrets, prefer the `--kiro-from-secret` / Secrets Manager pattern which flows only a reference.
</Warning>

## Deploying the template directly

```bash
aws cloudformation create-stack \
  --stack-name lowkey-myagent \
  --template-body file://deploy/cloudformation/template.yaml \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=PackName,ParameterValue=openclaw \
    ParameterKey=ProfileName,ParameterValue=builder \
    ParameterKey=EnvironmentName,ParameterValue=my-agent \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t4g.xlarge \
    ParameterKey=DefaultModel,ParameterValue=us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1 \
    ParameterKey=LokiWatermark,ParameterValue=my-agent \
    ...
```

For a builder-profile OpenClaw install this is ~17 parameters. Running `install.sh` is almost always easier.

## Customizing UserData

UserData is embedded in the template. It:

1. Installs `git` if missing
2. Clones the lowkey repo at `RepoBranch`
3. Calls `deploy/bootstrap.sh` with the pack + profile args
4. Signals CFN success/failure

If you fork the repo and want to run the installer from your fork, change `RepoBranch` (and update the `git clone` URL in the template).

## Watching the deploy

```bash
# Stack events
aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name lowkey-myagent \
  --query 'StackEvents[?ResourceStatus==`CREATE_IN_PROGRESS` || ResourceStatus==`CREATE_FAILED`]'

# Bootstrap progress — published to SSM Parameter Store
aws ssm get-parameter --name /loki/setup-step --query Parameter.Value
aws ssm get-parameter --name /loki/setup-status --query Parameter.Value
```

Bootstrap publishes `/loki/setup-status` = `IN_PROGRESS|COMPLETE|FAILED` and `/loki/setup-step` = human-readable current step.

## Tear-down

```bash
aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name lowkey-myagent
```

The stack owns the VPC, EC2, IAM — everything vanishes together. Security service subscriptions may linger briefly (AWS detaches them async).

<Info>
If you reused an existing VPC via `ExistingVpcId`, the VPC is **not** deleted (you brought it, you keep it).
</Info>
